title: Bachelor of Computer Science
description: A Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (BSCS) is a 3-4 year undergraduate degree focused on computing fundamentals, software development, algorithms, and data structures. It prepares students for high-demand careers like software engineering, data science, and cybersecurity. The curriculum blends theoretical knowledge with practical skills, often requiring a capstone project.

title: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
description: BSN trains students in patient care, clinical practice, anatomy, physiology, and medical ethics. Clinical rotations are mandatory. Graduates can work as Registered Nurses in hospitals, clinics, and community health centers.

title: Bachelor of Information Technology
description: BSIT emphasizes networking, web development, database management, cloud computing, and IT infrastructure. It prepares students for roles such as Network Administrator, Web Developer, IT Support Specialist, and Systems Administrator.

title: Bachelor of Business Administration
description: BSBA covers finance, marketing, human resource management, accounting, and entrepreneurship. Specializations may include Marketing, Finance, HRM, or Management. Graduates pursue careers as Business Managers, Financial Analysts, Marketing Executives, or Entrepreneurs.

title: Bachelor of Civil Engineering
description: BSCE focuses on construction, structural design, transportation systems, environmental engineering, and geotechnical studies. Students learn project management and site supervision. Career paths include Civil Engineer, Construction Manager, and Structural Designer.

title: Bachelor of Science in Psychology
description: This program studies human behavior, mental health, cognitive processes, and research methods. Graduates may work in HR departments, counseling centers, research organizations, or continue to clinical psychology at the master’s level.

title: Associate Degree Program in Information Technology
description: The Associate Degree Program (ADP) in Information Technology is a 2-year, 4-semester undergraduate program designed to equip students with foundational skills in software development, networking, and system administration. It prepares graduates for entry-level roles like IT support, web development, or junior programming.

title: Associate Degree Program in Computer Science
description: A 2-year Computer Science Associate Degree (typically 60-80 credits) provides foundational knowledge in programming, databases, and IT systems, preparing students for entry-level tech jobs or transfer to a bachelor's program. Often offered as an Associate of Science (AS) or Applied Science (AAS), this 4-semester, market-driven degree is available at community colleges and universities.

title: Associate Degree Program in Commerce
description: An Associate Degree in Commerce (ADC) is a 2-year (four-semester) undergraduate program, equivalent to 14 years of study, focusing on practical skills in accounting, finance, economics, and business law. It serves as a replacement for traditional B.Com degrees, providing immediate employability for entry-level roles or a pathway to transfer into the 5th semester of a 4-year BS/BBA program.

title: Associate Degree Program in Arts
description: An Associate Degree in Arts (ADA) is a 2-year, 4-semester undergraduate program, equivalent to the traditional B.A., focusing on humanities, social sciences, and specialized subjects like English, Psychology, or Economics. It bridges intermediate education and 4-year BS degrees, designed to enhance skills for immediate employment or further study.

title: Associate Degree Program in English
description: Associate Degree Program (ADP) in English is a two-year, 4-semester undergraduate program, equivalent to 14 years of education, focused on building foundational skills in English literature, linguistics, composition, and critical analysis. It prepares students for careers in writing, media, and education, or allows them to bridge directly into the 3rd year of a BS English program.
